description: Knowledge Context Pack V1.1 for CER Review Assist Sandbox Pilot. Extends V1 with evidence depth taxonomy, MDR transitional provisions, standards manifest, device class patterns, and external dependency taxonomy. Preserves all V1 calibration assets.
delta_from_v1: Added MDR Article 120 transitional provisions reference data
Added harmonized standards manifest (5 core standards)

evidence_depth_taxonomy.description: Four-level evidence depth classification for all findings. Required field on every finding across all agents.

PRIMARY_VERBATIM.definition: Verbatim excerpt from an original source document (CER, IFU, RMF, SSCP, BSI letter, etc.)
PRIMARY_VERBATIM.source_example: Direct quote from CER Section 4.2: 'The device demonstrated 98% first-shock success...'

PRIMARY_DERIVED.definition: Content derived/paraphrased from original source but traceable to specific section
PRIMARY_DERIVED.source_example: Summary of IFU Section 3 warnings, paraphrased with section reference

SECONDARY_SUMMARY.definition: Excerpt from Track B agent-generated summary (INTAKE_REPORT, PROJECT_PROFILE, etc.)
SECONDARY_SUMMARY.required_action: Finding MUST state: 'Verify against primary source at [source path]. Current evidence is from [Track B file] which is an agent-generated summary.'
SECONDARY_SUMMARY.human_gate: Required for MEDIUM severity; finding auto-capped at MEDIUM

MISSING_PRIMARY.definition: Primary source not accessible to sandbox; evidence from indirect reference only
MISSING_PRIMARY.source_example: Content referenced in Track B summary but original file not in sandbox input
MISSING_PRIMARY.required_action: Classify as UNVERIFIED_GAP. Recommendation MUST include: 'Verify against [specific document] at [source path] before finalizing.'
MISSING_PRIMARY.human_gate: Conditional — only if severity would otherwise be MEDIUM+

CRITICAL.rule: CRITICAL severity requires PRIMARY_VERBATIM evidence — no exceptions

HIGH.rule: HIGH severity requires PRIMARY evidence — no exceptions

MEDIUM.rule: SECONDARY_SUMMARY evidence caps severity at MEDIUM

INFORMATIONAL.rule: INFORMATIONAL tier has no evidence depth requirement; not counted in gap metrics

auto_downgrade_rules: CRITICAL + SECONDARY_SUMMARY → downgrade to MEDIUM, add 'Verify against primary source'
CRITICAL + MISSING_PRIMARY → downgrade to LOW, reclassify as UNVERIFIED_GAP
HIGH + SECONDARY_SUMMARY → downgrade to MEDIUM, add 'Verify against primary source'
HIGH + MISSING_PRIMARY → downgrade to LOW, reclassify as UNVERIFIED_GAP
Missing evidence_depth field → default to MISSING_PRIMARY, downgrade to LOW

severity_framework_extended.description: V1.1 extends the V1.0 D7 severity framework with INFORMATIONAL tier.

examples: Minor phrasing notes without regulatory implications
INFORMATIONAL.gate: Auto-pass always; not counted in gap metrics

mdr_transitional_provisions.description: MDR Article 120 transitional provision reference for MDD/MDR boundary decisions.

conditions: Device has valid MDD certificate issued before 2021-05-26
No significant changes in design or intended purpose
Manufacturer meets MDR post-market surveillance requirements

valid_mdd_certificate.note: Device may operate under MDD per Article 120 transitional provisions

certificate_expired_or_none.note: MDD framework reference without valid transitional provision

certificate_status_unknown.note: Certificate status unknown — verify against manufacturer's MDD certificate before escalating

no_transitional_eligibility.note: MDR required — device not eligible for MDD transitional provisions

article_120.severity_guidance: If device has valid MDD certificate under Article 120, MDD framework reference is NOT a boundary violation. Flag as standards_currency_note at MEDIUM.

harmonized_standards_manifest.description: Current harmonized standards reference for systematic standards currency checking.

standards.title: Medical device software — Software life cycle processes
Medical electrical equipment — General requirements for basic safety

harmonized_standards_manifest.usage: For each standard referenced in project documents, check: (a) is the referenced version the current harmonized version? (b) are any published amendments missing from the reference? (c) has the standard been withdrawn or superseded?
harmonized_standards_manifest.limitation: Full standards manifest requires Context Pack V2. V1.1 includes 5 core standards only. For unlisted standards, flag as: 'Standards currency not verified — [standard] not in sandbox standards manifest.'

meddev_2_7_1_rev4_reference.description: MEDDEV 2.7/1 Rev.4 key guidance for benefit-risk assessment calibration.

benefit_risk_conclusion.key_point: A qualitative benefit-risk conclusion is acceptable when quantitative data supporting the conclusion exists elsewhere in the CER dossier.

QUALITATIVE_CONCLUSION_WITH_QUANTITATIVE.definition: Conclusion stated qualitatively but quantitative data exists elsewhere in the dossier
QUALITATIVE_CONCLUSION_WITH_QUANTITATIVE.action: Do NOT flag as a gap

UNSUPPORTED_CONCLUSION.definition: Conclusion lacks data backing anywhere in the dossier

benefit_risk_conclusion.severity_guidance: UNSUPPORTED_CONCLUSION (no data backing) → MEDIUM. QUALITATIVE_WITH_QUANTITATIVE_BACKING → INFORMATIONAL or no finding.

device_class_patterns.description: Device-class-specific regulatory concern patterns observed across pilots.

external_dependency_taxonomy.description: Three-state classification for external dependencies. Replaces binary present/absent from V1.0.

PRESENT.definition: File exists and is readable in the project bundle

EXTERNAL_CONFIRMED_HANDOFF.definition: File is external but handoff point is documented (traceability matrix reference, closeout confirmation of separate file)
examples: Risk management file maintained separately per QMS
NB review letter archived in separate regulatory dossier

MISSING.definition: File should be present but is not, and no handoff is documented

external_dependency_taxonomy.rule: Only MISSING contributes to negative completeness scoring. EXTERNAL_CONFIRMED_HANDOFF is a normal documentation pattern, not a gap.

project_type_classification_matrix.description: Project type classifier for determining CER review scope applicability.

CER_BUNDLE.definition: Full clinical evaluation report with clinical data and supporting documents
CER_BUNDLE.detection: Presence of CER, CEP, clinical evaluation, 临床评价 files

SOFTWARE_LIFECYCLE.scope_note: PROJECT_TYPE_SCOPE_NOTE — sandbox calibrated for CER review. Findings should be interpreted with scope limitation: this is a SOFTWARE_LIFECYCLE project, not a CER bundle.
SOFTWARE_LIFECYCLE.detection: EN 62304 references, software development/maintenance plans, SOUP list

RMF_ONLY.definition: Risk management file only, no CER
RMF_ONLY.detection: RMF present but no CER, no clinical data

MIXED.definition: Multiple document types including CER elements
MIXED.detection: Both CER and software lifecycle documents present

UNKNOWN.detection: None of the above patterns detected

pipeline_limitation_vs_document_quality.description: Distinction between pipeline constraints and actual document quality issues.

DOCUMENT_QUALITY_ISSUE.definition: Problem with the source document itself

PIPELINE_LIMITATION.definition: Constraint of the extraction/reading pipeline, not the document
examples: DOC format not readable by text extractor
PIPELINE_LIMITATION.action: Informational note only — does not reflect on document quality

severity_framework.INFORMATIONAL: Documentation polish — not counted in gap metrics (V1.1 NEW)
